target.reason: Fresh dispatch_latency profile showed out_axes=none constant vmap at 33.515 us while nearby BatchTrace constant_scalar_output was 2.7639 us, indicating loop-and-stack overhead for a batch-independent output.

lever.name: single_eval_batch_independent_out_axes_none
lever.description: When vmap_out_axes is all none and a static dataflow walk proves every output is independent of batched inputs, evaluate vmap(jit(f)) once at index 0 and allow tail-free BatchTrace to return unbatched outputs directly.

baseline.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-dispatch-latency-profile-pass30-20260603 rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-dispatch --bench dispatch_baseline -- dispatch_latency --sample-size 100 --measurement-time 3 --warm-up-time 1

after.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-fj-dispatch-outaxes-none-bench-pass32b rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-dispatch --bench dispatch_baseline -- dispatch_latency/vmap/constant_scalar_out_axes_none --sample-size 100 --measurement-time 3 --warm-up-time 1

isomorphism_proof.output_arity: preserved: the fast path returns the same result vector from execute_with_transforms or the same unbatched BatchTrace outputs.
isomorphism_proof.output_order: preserved: outputs are emitted in root_jaxpr.outvars/result order without sorting or reordering.
isomorphism_proof.dataflow_guard: fast path is enabled only when every outvar is proven independent of batched invars by a conservative equation-order walk; unknown vars, effects, sub-jaxprs, and unsupported primitives fall back.
isomorphism_proof.tie_breaking: none in target; no ordering-sensitive comparisons are introduced.
isomorphism_proof.floating_point: target golden uses integer literals; for supported elementwise floating-point graphs the guard does not reorder equations, and vmap(jit) single-eval is only used for batch-independent outputs.
isomorphism_proof.rng: none; effects or sub-jaxprs disable the fast path.
isomorphism_proof.fallbacks: batched or mixed out_axes, varying outputs, composed non-jit tails, non-elementwise primitives, effects, sub-jaxprs, and unsupported axes retain existing loop-and-stack or error behavior.
